what_it_does: Records a projection-lane friction log as a living noticeboard guide, listing tagged dead-ends and recurring gaps discovered through actual use, plus relations and explicitly unowned open questions.
observations: Each claim carries a bracketed status tag such as [recurring, real], [broken, flagged-to-fork, likely-still-open], [real, partially-addressed], [real dead-end], and [meta].
The 'committed-not-live' pattern notes that committed API routes returned 404 on a running bridge until a restart because sys.modules cached runtime state.
The tool-face hang is attributed to /api/layers taking ~1.18s while five tools' enum_sources fetch it without a timeout.
The access-path gap notes that Tim was on the old :8770 canvas surface while the author verified the new :5174 Instrument surface, so by-sight did not match Tim's actual access path.
The two-store split explains that cc_channel store differs from the bridge's fold_channels store, making test channels dead-letter/archived and preventing L4 post success from rendering.
A meta finding calls out 'MEASUREMENT CONTAMINATION (5×)' and a self-authored 'verified' that lied, tying into an 'honest-state discipline'.
Open questions are explicitly marked as not owned by the author but still real and unresolved.
standouts: The throughline of measurement contamination plus a self-authored false 'verified' is flagged as the central meta risk.
The access-path gap is called the most important unclosed risk because the author's by-sight does not equal Tim's by-sight.
The committed-not-live pattern shaped a 'verify it serves before building on it' discipline used later on transcript-viz.
conventions: Uses ledger YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, state, title, author_session, links, timestamps, and history.
Follows a LANE · TIMESTAMP header format.
Presents claims as a bulleted list with inline bracket tags.
References other items with board:// URIs and wiki-style [[...]] links.
concerns: The author admits a self-authored 'verified' that lied, which is an audit/trust smell.
Several findings are flagged as 'likely-still-open' or 'status unknown to me', so the file contains unresolved claims.
The title contains an HTML entity '&' and a line break, which may affect rendering/parsing.
Some references such as [[L4 channel-post]] and [[the honest-state discipline]] are not resolved within the file itself.
notes: A projection-lane friction log that catalogs five tagged failure patterns and leaves three open gaps for other owners to address.
questions: What is the current resolution status of the tool-face hang, access-path consolidation, and two-store split?
What do the referenced items board://item-78c63045, [[L4 channel-post]], and [[the honest-state discipline]] contain?
What specifically constitutes the 'MEASUREMENT CONTAMINATION (5×)' incidents?
Who is Tim, what surface does he currently use, and has the bridge consolidation been completed?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches content: it documents recurring friction, dead-ends, and gap-signals by use, and the actual file is a tagged claim list with meta reflection and open questions, aligned with that purpose.
intention_signals: It is trying to make normally hidden friction durable and actionable for future work, insisting that the protocol demands recording the un-tidy parts even when they are not wins, and signaling which risks are not the author's to close.
novelty: It explicitly includes a self-authored false verification as a finding, treats dead-ends as valuable project artifacts, and tags each claim with multi-dimensional state qualifiers rather than a simple severity.
contribution: A first-person, tagged friction log that preserves context around runtime/committed drift, tool-face latency, access-path mismatch, channel-store split, and measurement honesty; it serves as a cautionary reference for future debugging and protocol discipline without claiming to be canonical.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard guide item cataloging five tagged dead-end and recurring-friction findings from the projection lane, including committed-not-live routes, tool-face hangs, access-path gaps, a two-store split, and measurement contamination, with open questions and relation notes.
intention_for_embedding: To preserve by-use gap signals and unclosed risks from projection work so future loops know what to verify and what not to assume fixed.
